sections: A Single Coordinator Who Owns Your Whole Relocation

Every commercial move we handle is run by one named project lead from the very first site survey through to the final box being unpacked at your new premises. That single point of contact removes the confusion that derails so many office relocations, where instructions get lost between facilities managers, IT teams and removal crews. Your coordinator builds the master plan, books the vehicles and labour, liaises with your landlord or building manager, and stays reachable on the day so decisions are made in minutes rather than hours. Because we are a family-run Sussex business founded in 2016, you deal with people who treat your deadline as their own. The same coordinator who walks your floor plate at the survey is the one directing the move, so nothing is filtered through a call centre. If you want to understand how we work before committing, our team and company background sets out exactly who you will be dealing with, and you can request a commercial relocation quote once the scope is clear.
Mapping Downtime to the Hour Before We Lift a Thing

The cost of a commercial move is rarely the removal itself — it is the productive hours your business loses while desks sit empty. Our planning starts by working backwards from the moment you need to be trading or operational again, then sequencing every task so the critical path is as short as possible. We identify which departments can move first, which systems must stay live until the last minute, and where temporary overlap between old and new sites keeps customer-facing functions running. Phased moves are often the answer for larger organisations, shifting one floor or team at a time across consecutive evenings so no single day brings the business to a halt.

Detailed scheduling like this is only possible with the right resources behind it, and we plan each phase around clear deliverables:

  • A written move timeline with start and finish windows for every department
  • Decant and reinstatement sequencing so priority teams are productive first
  • Contingency slots built in for lift downtime, traffic or access delays
  • A go or no-go checkpoint the night before so surprises are caught early

For most businesses the cleanest relocation happens when the office is empty. We routinely run evening, overnight and weekend moves so that staff leave on Friday from one address and arrive Monday at another that is already set up and switched on. Working out of hours means we can take our time with delicate equipment, use goods lifts without competing with other tenants, and avoid the building’s peak-traffic restrictions. It also means your team never loses a working day to packing and waiting around. We schedule crews and vehicles specifically for these windows rather than treating them as an afterthought, and our coordinator confirms building access, security sign-in and parking suspensions in advance so the crew is never left standing at a locked loading bay. Where a move spans a wider area, our coverage across West Sussex and the surrounding counties means we can stage labour and vehicles close to both the outgoing and incoming sites.
Desk Crate Systems, Colour Coding & Labelling

A commercial relocation lives or dies on its labelling. We supply secure, stackable plastic crates rather than cardboard for office moves, because they protect contents better, stack cleanly on trolleys and are far quicker to load and unload. Each member of staff is given crates tagged to their name and their destination desk, so when crates land at the new site they go straight to the right position with no second-guessing. We operate a colour-coded floor-plan system: every zone on the new layout has a colour, every crate and item of furniture carries a matching label, and the crew works from a master plan posted at the destination entrance. That discipline is what lets a hundred-desk floor be reinstated in a single weekend. For specialist or oversized items that crates cannot protect, our bespoke crate-building service produces made-to-measure cases, and a full professional packing service is available for teams who would rather keep working while we handle the boxing.

Technology is the part of any office move where mistakes are most expensive, so we treat it as a distinct workstream. We coordinate closely with your IT team or provider on decommission and recommission timing, label and bag cables to their workstations, and transport servers, network racks, monitors and workstations in purpose-built protective packaging. Sensitive hardware travels in air-ride or padded vehicles where appropriate and is handled by crew briefed specifically on that equipment rather than treated like general furniture. We can move kit in the exact sequence your IT team needs to bring systems back online, prioritising the server room and core network so connectivity is restored first and staff log straight in on Monday morning. The same care extends to specialist commercial equipment — medical, laboratory, print, retail point-of-sale and audio-visual gear — all of which we crate, brace and position to manufacturer-friendly standards. As a fully insured operator carrying £10m liability cover, we give you the documented assurance that high-value equipment is protected in transit.

Not every commercial move is a desk-based office. We relocate retail units, stockrooms, warehouses and document archives, each of which carries its own challenges. Retail moves are planned around trading hours so shelving, stock and fixtures transfer overnight and the shop reopens without lost sales. Warehouse relocations demand careful handling of racking, pallets and bulk stock, with stock counts and condition checks built into the process so nothing goes missing between sites. Filing and archive moves are sequenced and labelled box by box so records stay in order and remain retrievable throughout, which matters enormously where retention and audit obligations apply. Where there is a gap between vacating one site and occupying the next, our business and commercial storage bridges it with secure space for furniture, stock or archives. For clients with ongoing distribution needs, our contract delivery service can continue moving goods between locations once the initial relocation is complete.

Delicate, heavy or high-value items that ordinary cartons cannot protect are crated to measure. Our custom crate service builds bespoke timber cases and foam-lined inserts shaped to fit machinery, laboratory and print equipment, framed artwork, mirrors, glass and signage, while professional packing wraps and cushions everything that travels alongside them. Awkward, weighty pieces are moved with stair-climbers, skates, lifting straps and proper manual-handling technique, so neither the goods nor the building’s floors, walls and doorframes are damaged in the process. Each crate is inventoried and labelled to its destination, and we can hold items in secure business storage for stock, furniture or archives during a phased move or refurbishment, returning them in the same condition they left.

Commercial premises bring access constraints that a domestic move never does — shared loading bays, restricted lift dimensions, fire-door routes, height-limited car parks and tight delivery windows imposed by building management. We survey all of this before the move and produce a method statement and risk assessment so the crew, your facilities team and the building manager all work from the same plan. Stair runs, lift weight limits and protective floor and corner coverings are agreed in advance, and we book parking suspensions or loading-bay slots where they are needed. This planning is not box-ticking; it is what keeps people safe, protects the building’s fabric so you recover your deposit, and prevents the delays that arise when a crew arrives to find the lift booked out or the bay blocked. Our Checkatrade-verified record and LAPADA membership reflect the standards we hold ourselves to on every job.
faqs: How do you keep business downtime to a minimum during an office move?

We plan the relocation backwards from the moment you need to be operational again, then sequence the work so the critical path is as short as possible. For most clients that means an out-of-hours, weekend or phased move — staff leave one site and return to another that is already set up. Your dedicated coordinator confirms building access, lift availability and IT timings in advance so no time is lost on the day, and contingency slots are built into the schedule to absorb any delays.
Can you move our IT equipment and servers safely?

Yes. IT is handled as a separate workstream in close coordination with your IT team or provider. We label and bag cables, transport servers, racks, monitors and workstations in purpose-built protective packaging, and move equipment in the sequence needed to bring core systems and connectivity back online first. As a fully insured company carrying £10m liability cover, we provide documented assurance that your high-value hardware is protected in transit.
Do you work evenings and weekends so we don’t lose a working day?

We do — out-of-hours, overnight and weekend moves are a core part of our commercial service rather than an exception. Working when your premises are empty lets us use goods lifts freely, take extra care with delicate equipment and avoid peak-traffic restrictions, so your team arrives to a fully set-up office without losing a productive day.
What if there’s a gap between leaving our old premises and moving into the new one?

That is common with commercial leases, and we bridge it with secure business and commercial storage. We can hold furniture, stock or archives for as long as you need and deliver everything to the new site once it is ready, all coordinated by the same project lead who managed the move so nothing falls between the cracks.
